pages.full_text:
both enjoyed the gold mine. We had lunch in the carpark. The engine shed showed old air compressors & electric start-up machines & a gantry. The Romans did open cast mining & collected water in huge pools (size of swimming pools) released it in one go & water scoured the hill side. They built 12 day fires against the limestone & cold water, cracks, pickaxes. Slaves worked 3 water wheel tread mills to pump water out of mines. Then 1890, Then 1930s, but poison in cold, purified in Hamburg before WWII or Canada, but eventually flooded. Reopend by the university students. Carat measurement from ~~cart~~ carob seeds. Johne - VC in India Mutiny but shot by his butler.
     We drove on to see a bridge railway via duct. It was under
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from a previous page — no page number visible at top. The word 'cart' appears struck through before 'carob seeds'. 'Reopend' appears to be a spelling as written. 'Johne' may be a name abbreviation (possibly 'John'). 'railway via duct' likely means 'railway viaduct'. The text ends mid-sentence, continuing on the right page.

pages.full_text:
scaffolding. We had to come back to Clanwrthol campsite because Simon ha[d] left coffee pot.
     Rain off + on all day. Dog patient.
Sunday
Gloomy morning. Rain. We drove over to the Elan Valley again + read in the van until the rain stopped at 12. Then we had a super bike ride. The rain only started again just as we got back!
We cycled from a bridge near a church along a cycle track that had been a railway for building the dams. We reached Pen y Garreg Dam which holds 1300 mill gall - enough to give B'ham water for 15 days.
     We had [lunch] tea in a tearoom in a house on top of a hillock, the road in a cut-away next to it. A very nice ride.
Returning at 2.30, we had tea + a slow drive back to B'ham.
pages.notes: Page number 74 visible at top-right. 'Clanwrthol' may be a phonetic spelling — possibly Claerwen or Elan Valley campsite; could also read 'Clanworthol' or similar Welsh name. 'B'ham' is abbreviation for Birmingham. The word 'lunch' appears to be written above 'tea' as a correction or annotation (superscript). 'Pen y Garreg Dam' is in the Elan Valley, Wales. No year is directly stated on this page; context (Elan Valley, Birmingham visitors) is consistent with a Wales camping trip. Year cannot be anchored from this page alone.
